subject: [PATCH v1 14/17] fuse: use passthrough getattr in setattr suid/sgid handling

reviews.author: Amir Goldstein
reviews.summary:
The reviewer suggested enforcing a dependency between fc->handle_killpriv and fc->handle_killpriv_v2, as well as passthrough of SETATTR and possibly GETATTR operations, to ensure reliable suid/sgid killing in the userspace filesystem.

Reviewer Amir Goldstein expressed uncertainty about the logic of only passing through setattr operations when passthrough getattr is enabled, suggesting it might not be correct to do so.
reviews.raw_body:
/*
                 * The only sane way to reliably kill suid/sgid is to do it in
                 * the userspace filesystem
                 *
                 * This should be done on write(), truncate() and chown().
                 */
                if (!fc->handle_killpriv && !fc->handle_killpriv_v2) {

We should probably enforce a dependency between those two and
passthrough of SETATTR and maybe GETATTR - not sure what
the rules should be though.

---

It does not feel right to passthrough GETATTR if not passthrough SETATTR
in this case - I could be wrong.

Thanks,
Amir.

reviews.author: Joanne Koong (author)
reviews.summary:
Author considered Amir's suggestion to skip manual stripping if setattr is passed through and proposed an alternative approach

Author acknowledges that adding a check is necessary, but expresses hesitation about implementing it and suggests it might be the server's responsibility.
reviews.raw_body:
Not sure if this is what you had in mind, but skip the manual
stripping entirely if setattr is passed through since the backing
filesystem will handle it? eg

  if (!fc->handle_killpriv && !fc->handle_killpriv_v2 &&
      !fuse_inode_passthrough_op(inode, FUSE_SETATTR)) {

---

My mental model was that if a server wishes to pass through getattr
but not setattr or vice versa, they are responsible for keeping the
state in sync with the backing file. I'm happy to add the check in
though if you think it's better to add this.

Thanks,
Joanne

reviews.author: Amir Goldstein
reviews.summary:
Reviewer Amir Goldstein expressed concern that the patch is not necessary because the PASSTHROUGH_INO feature would handle suid/sgid kill in a different way, requiring either server-side handling or native handling on passthrough write/trunc ops, and suggested that the explicit non-atomic code in this patch could be avoided.

Reviewer Amir Goldstein expressed concern that the patch may not be necessary if the inode has passthrough getattr enabled, and suggested avoiding this code path altogether.
reviews.raw_body:
I was hoping that this will not be needed because
PASSTHROUGH_INO feature will require HANDLE_KILLPRIV_V2
and then either server handles killpriv or killpriv happens natively
on passthrough write/trunc ops, but never need this explicit non
atomic code to run.

---

Though question - I am hoping that we can avert it by never reaching
this code with PASSTHROUGH_INO enabled.

Thanks,
Amir.

2026-04-20.patch_summary: This patch modifies the behavior of setattr in fuse when handling suid/sgid permissions. When refreshing inode mode, it uses passthrough getattr if the inode has that capability enabled. This change ensures that stale i_mode values are not used during ia_mode calculation.
